data.title: Year-1 may hit a building lull the trend growth hides
data.narrative: The model assumes Year-1 lands in an average demand year at 1% of the serviceable market. But EMEA capacity under construction has stalled at about 2.5 GW while the pipeline grew 25% to nearly 15 GW, so projects are stuck in planning. If 2026 is a digestion year, Year-1 order pace could be roughly half the assumed level.

mechanism.text: EMEA construction stalled at 2.5 GW; pipeline grew to 15 GW
Cloud buys 61% of demand; a pause stalls orders
A trough year roughly halves achievable Year-1 pace

data.whyMissable: The growth rate looks solid across three publishers, but a smooth average hides which year of the cycle the launch actually lands in.
data.falsifier: Cushman & Wakefield or JLL H1-2026 prints showing under-construction capacity above 2.5 GW with rising starts, or hyperscaler guidance re-accelerating 2026 European data-center capex.
data.likelihoodRationale: The plateau and buyer concentration are documented, but the timing of a digestion year is a judgment call. Record 2026 hyperscaler leasing and an 83% pre-let pipeline point the other way, so we cut the estimate modestly from 0.35.
